title: Tomato Egg Drop Soup

ingredients.title: Soup Base
items: 4 medium ripe tomatoes, peeled and diced (or 3 large ones)
1 small piece ginger (about 1 inch), finely sliced

ingredients.title: Egg Drop
items: 1 tsp cornstarch + 1 tbsp water (optional, for silky texture)

ingredients.title: Garnish
items: A few drops of sesame oil or chili oil (optional)

instruction.title: Prepare the Tomatoes
steps: Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water for 30 seconds, then transfer to cold water to peel easily.
Dice the tomatoes into small, even pieces.

instruction.title: Make the Soup Base
steps: Heat a wok or saucepan over medium heat, add a teaspoon of oil.
Sauté the garlic and ginger until fragrant (about 30 seconds).
Add the diced tomatoes, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Cook until softened and juicy (3–4 minutes).
Pour in chicken stock, bring to a gentle boil, then simmer for 5–7 minutes.
Adjust seasoning with soy sauce, white pepper, and salt if needed.

instruction.title: Prepare the Egg Drop
steps: Optional: Mix cornstarch with water and add to eggs for silkier texture.
Slowly pour egg mixture into simmering soup while stirring gently in one direction to form ribbons.

instruction.title: Final Touches
steps: Turn off heat and stir in sesame oil.
Serve hot, garnished with spring onions, coriander, or chili oil.

chefTips: Use ripe tomatoes for natural sweetness and better color.
Strain soup if you prefer a smoother texture.
Pour eggs slowly while stirring to create silky ribbons.
Adjust acidity with a pinch of sugar if tomatoes are too sour.
Homemade chicken stock significantly improves flavor.
